name: Thyme Focaccia and Parmesan Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon table salt (about 5 1/2 cups all-purpose flour)
freshly ground black pepper for sprinkling
recipeInstructions:
1. In a standing electric mixer fitted with paddle attachment, beat together yeast, sugar, and water until foamy. Let stand 5 minutes, or until foamy.
2. In a bowl, stir together table salt and 5 cups flour. Stir oil into yeast mixture.
3. With the motor on low speed, gradually add flour mixture to yeast mixture. Knead dough with the dough hook for 2 minutes, or until soft and slightly sticky.
4. Transfer dough to a floured surface and knead in enough remaining flour to form a soft but not sticky dough. Form dough into a ball and place in an oiled large bowl, turning to coat. Cover bowl with a kitchen towel and let dough rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 45 minutes.
5. Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface and divide in half. Knead thyme into one half and knead the plain half for 1 minute. Form each half into an oval and invert bowl over them. Let dough rest 5 minutes for easier rolling.
6. Preheat oven to 450°F.
7. Oil two 13- by 9-inch baking pans and sprinkle each with 2 tablespoons cornmeal.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out dough halves into 13- by 9-inch rectangles using a floured rolling pin. Fit the rectangles into pans. Cover each pan with a kitchen towel and let dough rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 20 minutes.
9. Sprinkle plain dough with Parmesan cheese and sprinkle both doughs with coarse salt and pepper. Make indentations, about 1/2 inch deep and 1 inch apart, all over the dough rectangles using lightly oiled fingertips.
10. Bake focaccia in the middle of the oven for 12 minutes, or until golden. Remove focaccia from pans and cool on racks. Makes 1 thyme focaccia and 1 Parmesan focaccia. If you need only one focaccia, freeze the other for later use. Focaccia keeps, frozen, for 2 weeks.
